Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And First Amended Summons
Off Calendar Aug-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MICHAEL KAMBIE's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And First Amended Summons

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file an amended complaint is granted and denied in part.

Plaintiff has leave to amend to identify Daniel Chen as the partner referenced in paragraph 6 of the original complaint. There is no prejudice in allowing the partner to be expressly named in the amended complaint.

Plaintiff is denied leave to amend to add Mr. Chen as a co-plaintiff. The proposed amended complaint admits that Mr. Chen discovered the alleged negligence in May of 2021. (Berokim Decl., Ex 2 [Amended Complaint, par. 12].) Mr. Chen therefore had until May of 2022 to assert an individual claim for attorney malpractice against defendant. (See CCP 340.6 ["An action against an attorney for a wrongful act or omission, other than for actual fraud, arising in the performance of professional services shall be commenced within one year after the plaintiff discovers, or through the use of reasonable diligence should have discovered, the facts constituting the wrongful act or omission, or four years from the date of the wrongful act or omission, whichever occurs first."].) Plaintiff does not suggest that there are any facts that he or Mr. Chen can allege that would toll the statute of limitations under CCP 340.6(a)(1) - (4). "Leave to amend should be denied where the facts are not in dispute and the nature of the claim is clear, but no liability exists under substantive law." (Lawrence v. Bank of America (1985) 163 Cal.App.3d 431, 436; Foxborough v. Van Atta (1994) 26 Cal.App.4th 217, 230 [proposed amendment barred by statute of limitations and no basis for "relation back"].)

"Whether an amendment of a pleading will be allowed to change the description or characterization from an individual, a partnership or other association, after the statute of limitations has run depends on whether the misdescription or mischaracterization is merely a misnomer or defect in the description or characterization, or whether it is a substitution or entire change of parties. In the former case an amendment will be allowed; in the latter, it will not be allowed." (Kerr-Mcgee Chemical Corp. v. Superior Court (1984) 160 Cal.App.3d 594, 599.) California law provides that where an additional party plaintiff seeks to join a pending timely-filed complaint and seeks to enforce an independent right, the amended pleading does not relate back to the original complaint. (See Bartalo v. Superior Court (1975) 51 Cal.App.3d 526, 533 [denying leave to amend to plead loss of consortium claim].) Based on the above, Mr. Chen's attempt to assert an independent individual claim for attorney malpractice is time-barred.

The court notes that factual issues may remain regarding (1) the scope of defendant's representation, (2) the relationship among plaintiff, Mr. Cheng, and defendant, and (3) whether plaintiff can recover on both loans or only one. (See Stroymeyer Decl., Ex. 1 [Chen's promissory note].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Curtis E Karnow, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/CEK)
